plan_title: CommonPlace chat surface plus accounts page
scope_note: The user narrowed execution to the CommonPlace chat and accounts pages in the deployment repo. Full graph-native conversation persistence remains a compatibility target and must not be reported complete unless the substrate API exists and is wired.

items.title: D1. Conversation and message as graph-native objects
items.implementation_slice: Define the frontend chat message shape so it can map cleanly to future Conversation and Message Items, but do not create a parallel backend model in travisgilbert.me.
items.acceptance_criterion: The deployed Chat surface has typed conversation/message records with role, content, timestamp, run id, and references fields, and no code claims graph-native persistence is complete without a real substrate endpoint.
validation: Typecheck or targeted eslint confirms message types are used by the view.
Manual code review confirms no mock transcript is rendered as real history.
verification_evidence: Implemented src/lib/commonplace-chat.ts with typed conversation/message fields including role, content, timestamp, binding id, tenant, run metadata, and references.
npm test -- src/lib/commonplace-chat.test.ts passed with 4 tests.
Scoped eslint passed for the chat/account/navigation files.

items.title: D2. The chat view over messages
items.implementation_slice: Mount a real CommonPlace Chat screen using existing assistant-ui primitives and a local external-store adapter, not a bespoke message renderer.
items.acceptance_criterion: Opening Chat from /commonplace renders the assistant-ui thread shell with no studio-mock-data dependency, and newly submitted messages appear through the runtime adapter.
validation: rg confirms CommonPlace chat does not import src/lib/studio-mock-data.ts.
Live or local /commonplace smoke confirms the Chat sidebar entry opens the thread.
verification_evidence: CommonPlace Chat is routed as ScreenType chat via src/lib/commonplace.ts and src/components/commonplace/panes/ScreenRouter.tsx.
src/components/commonplace/views/CommonPlaceChatView.tsx mounts AssistantRuntimeProvider and useExternalStoreRuntime.
rg confirmed the new chat view does not import studio-mock-data.

items.title: D3. The turn as an object action that dispatches a harness job
items.implementation_slice: Dispatch chat turns through the existing Theorem agent product path instead of adding a new runner.
items.acceptance_criterion: Sending a Chat message calls runTheoremAgent via /api/theorem/agent, creates one user turn and one assistant result in the UI, and surfaces structured errors from the current endpoint.
validation: Targeted unit test or component test covers successful and failed runTheoremAgent calls.
Manual smoke confirms empty sends are disabled and backend failures remain visible.
verification_evidence: CommonPlaceChatView submits non-empty turns through runTheoremAgent with mode ask.
The existing /api/theorem/agent route remains the product fallback path through src/lib/theorem-agent.ts.
Error responses are appended as assistant messages with error status.

items.title: D4. No-cold-start context from the membrane
items.implementation_slice: Keep the UI contract ready to pass context metadata, but do not hand-roll membrane retrieval in the browser.
items.acceptance_criterion: The Chat request path preserves tenant and binding id controls already supported by runTheoremAgent and does not implement a second context assembly system client-side.
validation: Code review confirms no browser-side memory, graph, or file retrieval clone was introduced.
Request payload remains compatible with the current Theorem agent API.
verification_evidence: Chat requests preserve tenant Travis-Gilbert and a stable commonplace:chat binding id.
No browser-side memory, graph, or file retrieval clone was introduced.

items.title: D5. The streamed transcript with visible tool calls
items.implementation_slice: Render visible run and tool-audit metadata from the current Theorem agent response, with the UI isolated so a future SSE endpoint can replace the non-streaming adapter.
items.acceptance_criterion: A completed turn shows assistant text plus a visible run details block containing run id, heads, claims, evidence count, or alignment verdict when present; the UI does not fabricate tool calls when the endpoint does not return them.
validation: Fixture or mocked response test confirms run metadata renders.
Manual smoke confirms absent metadata renders as an honest empty state, not fake tool history.
verification_evidence: The Chat screen renders a Run Channel and Latest Run audit panel.
Run metadata is populated only from TheoremAgentRunResult fields: run id, heads, claims, evidence count, answer kind, and alignment verdict.
Absent metadata renders as an honest empty/not returned state.

items.title: D6. The conversation as a linked graph citizen
items.implementation_slice: Expose references and run metadata in the frontend model so future graph edges can attach without changing the UI shape.
items.acceptance_criterion: The Chat message type can carry referenced object ids and run ids, and the UI labels graph persistence as unavailable until a real conversation/message API is wired.
validation: Type-level check or unit test covers referenced object id metadata.
No UI text claims search/PPR graph citizenship unless backed by an API response.
verification_evidence: CommonPlaceChatRunMetadata includes runId and references for future graph attachment.
The UI does not claim persistent graph conversation storage; the checklist scope note keeps graph-native persistence deferred until a real endpoint exists.

items.title: Accounts page appended to CommonPlace Settings
items.spec_backreference: User addendum: tack accounts page onto the chat plan
items.implementation_slice: Add an Account or Accounts screen under Settings in the deployed CommonPlace sidebar.
items.acceptance_criterion: The /commonplace sidebar shows an Account or Accounts child under Settings, and clicking it opens a real CommonPlace account screen rather than the old placeholder.
validation: Live or local /commonplace smoke confirms Account appears under Settings.
No separate top-level /accounts route is required for the primary workflow.
verification_evidence: Accounts is a Settings child in SIDEBAR_SECTIONS and a CommonPlace rail item.
ScreenRouter renders AccountSettingsView for ScreenType accounts.
layout-provider maps #accounts to the accounts screen.

items.title: Accounts page uses existing owner auth
items.spec_backreference: User addendum: no separate account system
items.implementation_slice: Use NextAuth and the existing OwnerProvider state; do not hand-roll credentials, sessions, or token storage.
items.acceptance_criterion: The Account screen reports owner sign-in state and offers GitHub sign in or sign out through existing /api/auth routes, with no new custom auth backend.
validation: Targeted eslint/typecheck passes for auth-linked components.
Manual smoke confirms sign-in and sign-out controls navigate to existing NextAuth routes.
verification_evidence: AccountSettingsView consumes useOwner from OwnerProvider.
Sign in and sign out controls point to existing /api/auth/signin and /api/auth/signout routes.
No new credentials, session store, or custom auth backend was added.

items.title: GitHub App remains visible from accounts/settings
items.spec_backreference: Prior GitHub ingestion work plus user addendum
items.implementation_slice: Keep the existing GitHub App status card reachable from Settings or Account rather than burying it in a separate route.
items.acceptance_criterion: The existing GitHub App card still reads /api/theorem/github/status, shows webhook URL and setup state honestly, and remains reachable from the Settings or Account screen.
validation: curl /api/theorem/github/status returns 200 locally or on production.
Manual smoke confirms webhook and install status remain visible.
verification_evidence: SettingsView and AccountSettingsView share GithubAppPanel backed by useGithubAppStatus.
curl http://localhost:3127/api/theorem/github/status returned 200 with upstreamConfigured false and installUrl null.
The Settings child GitHub App remains mapped to the existing settings screen.

items.title: UI visual milestone for Chat and Accounts
items.implementation_slice: Capture baseline and target visual evidence before execution, then compare after implementation.
items.acceptance_criterion: Runtime complete, Product complete, and Vision complete are reported separately; before and after /commonplace screenshots plus the 21st.dev account reference are reviewed; the existing sidebar and split-pane workflow do not regress.
validation: Baseline screenshot captured or blocker recorded.
After screenshot reviewed at desktop and constrained viewport.
Do Not Downgrade gate passes before Product complete.
verification_evidence: Production build passed once before the final hash-link patch.
Local /commonplace returned 200 during dev smoke.
Playwright MCP failed because /Applications/Google Chrome.app was missing.
npx playwright install chrome required sudo; npx playwright install chromium hit ENOSPC.
The dev compile filled .next and had to be removed to restore disk space.
items.deferral_reason: Desktop/mobile screenshots could not be captured in this environment because browser automation was unavailable and the machine had only a few hundred MB free during validation.
